The '''Tech 4 PDV''', also known as '''Plexus Droid Vessels''' ('''PDV'''), were used by [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[Courier droid/Legends|courier droids]] which operated as communications and relay ships.

==Characteristics==
Staffed only by [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and minimally equipped, they were essentially engines and electronics. Used to support the [[Sector Plexus/Legends|Sector Plexus]], PDVs were designed to resemble [[mining droid]]s.<ref name="IS">''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''</ref>
They were capsule-shaped and could transport data across the galaxy.<ref name="DH">{{JournalCite|9|Death-Hunter}}</ref>

==History==
One such PDV was carrying information to an Imperial Intelligence [[Sector/Legends|sector]] headquarters when it was intercepted by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. In order to prevent its capture by the Rebels, the ship self-destructed after launching an [[I2-CG droid/Legends|I2-CG]] droid that was carrying the intelligence data. Despite the droid's self sacrifice, the Rebels detected the I2 unit descending to [[Goratak III]]. A skirmish ensued on the planet between the Rebel unit and an Imperial team led by [[Feskitt Bobb]].<ref name="MB">[[Star Wars Miniatures Battles (first edition)|''Star Wars Miniatures Battles'', First Edition]]</ref>

[[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Jesco Comark]] used these vessels during [[Operation Death-Hunter]]. In order to hide what was being manufactured by the project, the droids were used to transport the materials involved in the operation. The final product, [[Death-Hunter Cyborg]]s were transported aboard the courier droids.<ref name="DH" />
